channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Labor unveils major NDIS reforms amid criticism over access and delivery challenges
item.description: The article examines the Australian Labor government’s newly announced reforms to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), led by Health Minister Mark Butler, and the strong backlash they have...

item.title: Canadian Airlines Face Flight Reductions Amid Global Jet Fuel Shortages
item.description: Global airlines are experiencing significant flight cancellations due to a severe jet fuel shortage caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Although fewer than one per cent of Canadian flights...

item.title: Shaina Nitura makes UAAP history as Adamson's first women's volleyball MVP
item.description: Shaina Nitura of the Adamson Lady Falcons will make history as the first player from Adamson to be named Most Valuable Player (MVP) in the UAAP women's volleyball. After an outstanding Season 88 perfo...

item.title: House Justice Panel Sees Probable Cause to Impeach VP Duterte for Unexplained Wealth
item.description: Several members of the House justice committee believe there is probable cause to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte over allegations of unexplained wealth and violations in her Statement of Assets, ...

item.title: General MM Naravane clarifies stance on leadership and coordination during India-China standoff
item.description: The news article appears to focus on remarks made by former Indian Army Chief General M.M. Naravane regarding the India-China border standoff, particularly addressing claims that he was “abandoned” du...

item.title: Senior hospital executive arrested in ongoing investigation into Lucy Letby case
item.description: A senior healthcare boss from the Countess of Chester Hospital has been arrested by Cheshire Police on suspicion of perverting the course of justice. The arrest follows a search of a property as part ...

item.title: Kenyans React to Njugush Being Called 'Babe' by Influencer in Comedy Skits
item.description: Kenyan comedian Timothy Kimani, popularly known as Njugush, is trending online following his public confirmation of his split from wife Celestine Ndinda, also known as Wakavinye. The separation had be...

item.title: Lebanon and Israel face critical ceasefire talks amid regional tensions
item.description: Lebanon and Israel have agreed to a 10-day cessation of hostilities to allow peace negotiations, but the temporary truce is halfway through and tensions remain high. The deal does not require Israel t...

item.title: How Dry Ice Supports South Africa’s Cold Chain and Industrial Efficiency
item.description: Dry ice has become a key component in keeping South Africa’s industrial and logistics sectors running smoothly, particularly where strict temperature control and safe handling of sensitive goods are r...

item.title: Artisanal miners raise concerns over high environmental costs and safety practices in Singida
item.description: Artisanal and small-scale miners (ASMs) in Singida Region have raised strong concerns about the high cost of preparing mandatory Environmental Assessment Plans, saying the current system puts heavy pr...

item.title: Lulu Hassan Shows Her English Skills While Discussing AI Collaboration with Downy
item.description: Television presenter Lulu Hassan surprised many Kenyans recently when she spoke fluent English in a video shared on her Instagram. Known for her Kiswahili broadcasts, Lulu discussed a collaboration wi...

item.title: Errol Musk’s refugee claims face rebuke amid South Africa-Russia agricultural talks
item.description: Errol Musk recently drew international attention in Moscow by promoting the controversial 'white farmer genocide' narrative, suggesting that Russia should offer refugee status to South African white f...

item.title: Concerns over NDIS reforms and their impact on social participation for people with disabilities
item.description: This opinion piece argues that proposed reforms to Australia’s National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), announced by Health Minister Mark Butler, risk significantly harming people with disabilitie...

item.title: Congress criticises Modi for not responding to Trump's derogatory remarks on India
item.description: The Congress party has strongly criticised US President Donald Trump for sharing comments by American commentator Michael Savage that described India as a 'hellhole', linking it to immigration issues ...

item.title: US Embassy and Ilorin Innovation Hub sign MOU to boost tech and STEM education in Kwara
item.description: The US Embassy for Abuja don announce say dem don sign three-year Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Ilorin Innovation Hub. This na the first time dem dey run public-private partnership outside th...

item.title: Sara Cox to Host BBC Radio 2 Breakfast Show Following Scott Mills' Departure
item.description: Sara Cox has been appointed as the new host of BBC Radio 2's weekday Breakfast Show, succeeding Scott Mills. Mills, aged 53, was recently dismissed from the BBC following revelations of historical all...

item.title: Wildlife recovery in Chornobyl highlights complex legacy of nuclear disaster
item.description: Nearly four decades after the Chornobyl nuclear disaster, the exclusion zone remains heavily contaminated with radioactive materials such as caesium-137, plutonium and americium. Despite these hazards...

item.title: A Fully Randomized 2026 NFL Mock Draft Experiment and Its Most Realistic Fits
item.description: This article presents a lighthearted and unconventional take on the 2026 NFL Draft through a fully randomized mock draft. Created by Pride of Detroit’s Jeremy Reisman, the exercise continues a 13-year...

item.title: Pakistan to Gradually Transition National Transport to Electric Vehicles
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Thursday emphasised the need to gradually shift Pakistan’s transport sector to electric vehicles (EVs) as part of a broader energy security and sustainable development...

item.title: Trump Directs US Navy to Use Lethal Force Against Vessels Laying Mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: In a recent escalation of tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump issued a direct order to the US Navy via a Truth Social post. He commanded forces to 'shoot and kill any boat, small ...
